22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)

85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary report.

## Summary Report: vLLM Sampling Parameters Definition and Validation

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and found how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and used. Here are my findings:

### 1. **Main SamplingParams Class**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Key Structure:**
- Implemented as a `msgspec.Struct` (lines 118-122) with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True`
- Uses `@dataclass` decorator for post-initialization hooks

**Core Sampling Parameter Fields (lines 205-244):**
```
- n: int = 1 (Number of output sequences)
- best_of: Optional[int] = None
- presence_penalty: float = 0.0 (range: [-2, 2])
- frequency_penalty: float = 0.0 (range: [-2, 2])
- repetition_penalty: float = 1.0 (must be > 0)
- temperature: float = 1.0 (must be >= 0)
- top_p: float = 1.0 (range: (0, 1])
- top_k: int = 0 (must be >= 0 or -1)
- min_p: float = 0.0 (range: [0, 1])
- seed: Optional[int] = None
- stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
- stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
- ignore_eos: bool = False
- max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
- min_tokens: int = 0
- logprobs: Optional[int] = None
- prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
- detokenize: bool = True
- skip_special_tokens: bool = True
- spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
- logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
- include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
- truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
- output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
```

**Special Fields Set in post_init:**
```
- output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
- _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
- guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
- logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
- allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
- extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
- bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
- _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
```

### 2. **Validation of top_p and min_p Parameters**

**In `_verify_args()` method (lines 387-462):**

**top_p validation (line 417):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
```
- Enforces range: (0, 1] (exclusive 0, inclusive 1)

**min_p validation (lines 426-428):**
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.min_p}.")
```
- Enforces range: [0, 1] (inclusive on both ends)

**Special Handling in __post_init__ (lines 377-382):**
When temperature is near zero (greedy sampling):
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:  # _SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```
All sampling parameters are normalized to greedy defaults when temperature is near zero.

### 3. **SamplingTensors: GPU Kernel Data Structure**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Definition (lines 372-385):**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Data Extraction (lines 386-495):**
The `from_sampling_metadata()` classmethod builds these tensors from SamplingMetadata:
- Converts scalar parameters to batched tensors for GPU processing
- Returns tuple: `(SamplingTensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)`
- Pinned memory is used for CPU->GPU async transfer optimization

**min_p detection logic (lines 429-430):**
```python
if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:  # _SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
    do_min_p = True
```
Only applies min_p filtering when min_p is substantively non-zero.

### 4. **SequenceGroupToSample: Intermediate Data Structure**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Definition (lines 20-51):**
```python
@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams  # Carries the SamplingParams through the pipeline
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    query_len: Optional[int]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    is_prompt: bool
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    sample_indices: list[int]
```
This structure carries sampling params per sequence group through the processing pipeline.

### 5. **Sampling Metadata Wrapper**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata class (lines 97-196):**
- Holds `seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]`
- Also carries: `selected_token_indices`, `categorized_sample_indices`, `num_prompts`
- Uses `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` static method to construct from `SequenceGroupMetadata`

### 6. **min_p Implementation in GPU Sampling**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**_apply_min_p function (lines 427-441):**
```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

**Algorithm:**
1. Compute softmax probabilities from logits
2. Find maximum probability per batch element
3. Scale min_p threshold: `threshold = min_p * max_probability`
4. Mask tokens with probability below threshold to -inf (removes from consideration)

**Called in forward pass (lines 288-289):**
```python
if do_min_p:
    logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
```

### 7. **V1 Sampler SamplingMetadata**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**Structure (lines 12-44):**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```
Note: V1 does NOT have `min_p` as a tensor field in this metadata structure.

### 8. **Validation Constants**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/sampling_params.py`

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5  # Epsilon for floating-point comparison
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2      # Minimum allowed non-zero temperature
```

### Key Observations:

1. **top_p and min_p are distinct parameters:**
   - **top_p:** Nucleus sampling - selects top tokens with cumulative probability ≤ top_p
   - **min_p:** Minimum probability threshold - removes tokens below `min_p * max_probability`

2. **Parameter validation happens in two places:**
   - `SamplingParams.__post_init__()` - modifies parameters when temperature is near zero
   - `_verify_args()` - raises exceptions for invalid ranges

3. **GPU kernel data flow:**
   - `SamplingParams` → `SequenceGroupToSample` → `SamplingMetadata` → `SamplingTensors` → GPU kernels

4. **min_p is conditional:**
   - Only applied if `min_p > 1e-5` (via `do_min_p` flag)
   - Applied separately from top_k/top_p filtering

5. **All parameters are batched into tensors** for efficient GPU processing, preserving per-sequence parameter variation.

```
- Enforces range: (0, 1] (exclusive 0, inclusive 1)

**min_p validation (lines 426-428):**

```
- Enforces range: [0, 1] (inclusive on both ends)

**Special Handling in __post_init__ (lines 377-382):**
When temperature is near zero (greedy sampling):
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:  # _SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0

```
All sampling parameters are normalized to greedy defaults when temperature is near zero.

### 6. **min_p Implementation in GPU Sampling**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**_apply_min_p function (lines 427-441):**
```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

**Algorithm:**
1. Compute softmax probabilities from logits
2. Find maximum probability per batch element
3. Scale min_p threshold: `threshold = min_p * max_probability`
4. Mask tokens with probability below threshold to -inf (removes from consideration)

**Called in forward pass (lines 288-289):**
```python
if do_min_p:
    logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
```

### 8. **Validation Constants**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/vllm/sampling_params.py`

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5  # Epsilon for floating-point comparison
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2      # Minimum allowed non-zero temperature
```

### Key Observations:

1. **top_p and min_p are distinct parameters:**
   - **top_p:** Nucleus sampling - selects top tokens with cumulative probability ≤ top_p
   - **min_p:** Minimum probability threshold - removes tokens below `min_p * max_probability`

2. **Parameter validation happens in two places:**
   - `SamplingParams.__post_init__()` - modifies parameters when temperature is near zero
   - `_verify_args()` - raises exceptions for invalid ranges

3. **GPU kernel data flow:**
   - `SamplingParams` → `SequenceGroupToSample` → `SamplingMetadata` → `SamplingTensors` → GPU kernels

4. **min_p is conditional:**
   - Only applied if `min_p > 1e-5` (via `do_min_p` flag)
   - Applied separately from top_k/top_p filtering

5. **All parameters are batched into tensors** for efficient GPU processing, preserving per-sequence parameter variation.

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits
307

309	class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
310	
311	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
312	        super().__init__()
313	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
314	        self.device = device
315	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
316	
317	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
318	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
319	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))
320	
321	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
322	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
323	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
324	        return False
325	
326	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
327	        if not batch_update:
328	            return
329	
330	        # Process added requests.
331	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
332	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
333	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
334	                                                       params.logit_bias):
335	                self.biases[index] = lb
336	            else:
337	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
338	
339	        if self.biases:
340	            # Process removed requests.
341	            for index in batch_update.removed:
342	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
343	                    needs_update = True
344	
345	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
346	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
347	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
348	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
349	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
350	                            needs_update = True
351	                    else:
352	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
353	                        needs_update = True
354	                else:
355	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
356	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
357	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
358	                        needs_update = True
359	                    if a_entry is not None:
360	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
361	                        needs_update = True
362	
363	        # Update tensors if needed.
364	        if needs_update:
365	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
366	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
367	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
368	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
369	                biases.extend(lb.values())
370	
371	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
372	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
373	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
374

28	class CachedRequestState:
29	
30	    req_id: str
31	    prompt_token_ids: list[int]
32	    mm_inputs: list[MultiModalKwargs]
33	    mm_positions: list[PlaceholderRange]
34	    sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]
35	    pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams]
36	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
37	
38	    block_ids: tuple[list[int], ...]
39	    num_computed_tokens: int
40	    output_token_ids: list[int]
41	
42	    mrope_positions: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
43	    mrope_position_delta: Optional[int] = None
44	
45	    lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None
46	
47	    def __post_init__(self):
48	        self.num_prompt_tokens = len(self.prompt_token_ids)
49	
50	    @property
51	    def num_tokens(self) -> int:
52	        return self.num_prompt_tokens + len(self.output_token_ids)
53	
54	    def get_token_id(self, idx: int) -> int:
55	        if idx < self.num_prompt_tokens:
56	            return self.prompt_token_ids[idx]
57	        else:
58	            return self.output_token_ids[idx - self.num_prompt_tokens]

61	class InputBatch:
62	
63	    def __init__(
64	        self,
65	        max_num_reqs: int,
66	        max_model_len: int,
67	        max_num_batched_tokens: int,
68	        device: torch.device,
69	        pin_memory: bool,
70	        vocab_size: int,
71	        block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
72	        is_spec_decode: bool = False,
73	        logits_processing_needs_token_ids: bool = False,
74	    ):
75	        self.is_spec_decode = is_spec_decode
76	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs

78	        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
79	        self.device = device
80	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
81	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size

83	            logits_processing_needs_token_ids)
84	
85	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
86	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
87	
88	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
89	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
90	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
91	        # need to be pinned.
92	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
93	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
94	            device="cpu",
95	            dtype=torch.int32,
96	            pin_memory=False,
97	        )
98	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
99	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
100	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)

104	            device="cpu",
105	            dtype=torch.int32,
106	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
107	        )
108	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
109	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
110	
111	        # Block table.
112	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
113	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
114	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
115	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
116	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
117	            device=device,
118	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
119	        )
120	
121	        # Sampling-related.
122	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
123	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
124	                                       device=device)
125	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
126	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
127	                                                  device="cpu",
128	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
129	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
130	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
131	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
132	
133	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
134	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
135	                                 device=device)
136	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
137	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
138	                                            device="cpu",
139	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
140	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
141	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
142	
143	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
145	                                 device=device)
146	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
147	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
148	                                            device="cpu",
149	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
150	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()

## Summary: vLLM Sampling Parameters Data Flow

I've successfully traced how vLLM threads sampling parameters from the API request all the way through to the model executor. Here's the complete data flow:

### 1. **API Layer → SamplingParams (Entry Point)**

**Files:**
- `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` (lines 223-596 for ChatCompletionRequest, lines 741-981 for CompletionRequest)
- `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/serving_completion.py` (lines 75-180)

**Flow:**
1. OpenAI API request arrives as `ChatCompletionRequest` or `CompletionRequest` (Pydantic models)
2. These request objects have fields: `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`, `temperature`, etc.
3. Request calls `to_sampling_params()` method which converts API params to `SamplingParams`:
   - `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (line 452-543)
   - `CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (line 897-981)
4. The method uses `SamplingParams.from_optional()` factory method to create the internal structure

### 2. **Core SamplingParams Structure**

**File:** `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-589)

**Key Class:** `SamplingParams` (msgspec.Struct with `dict=True` and `omit_defaults=True`)

**Sampling Parameters Fields:**
```python
top_p: float = 1.0          # Line 212
top_k: int = 0              # Line 213
min_p: float = 0.0          # Line 214
temperature: float = 1.0    # Line 211
presence_penalty: float = 0.0
frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
```

**Conversion Methods:**
- `from_optional()` - Static factory method (lines 251-325) that converts optional API params to SamplingParams
- `__post_init__()` - Post-initialization validation and normalization (lines 327-385)
  - Sets `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0`, `min_p=0.0` when temperature is ~0 (greedy sampling)
  - Validates all parameters are within valid ranges

### 3. **Engine Layer → SequenceGroup Creation**

**File:** `/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py`

**Flow:**
1. Engine receives prompt + `SamplingParams` via `generate()` or `beam_search()` API
2. Creates a `SequenceGroup` with the `SamplingParams` (line 593-595)
3. Scheduler picks up `SequenceGroupMetadata` containing `sampling_params`

### 4. **Scheduler → Sampling Metadata Preparation**

**Files:**
- `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 97-369)
- `/vllm/core/scheduler.py`

**Flow:**
1. `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` (line 149-188) extracts sampling params from `SequenceGroupMetadata`
2. `_prepare_seq_groups()` (line 198-369) creates `SequenceGroupToSample` objects with `sampling_params` field
3. Key extraction point - Lines 408-417:
   ```python
   for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
       sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
       temperature = sampling_params.temperature
       top_p = sampling_params.top_p
       min_p = sampling_params.min_p
       top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
   ```

### 5. **Sampling Tensors Conversion (Critical Bridge)**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-561)

**Class:** `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 387-495)

**This is where individual SamplingParams fields become GPU tensors:**
- Lines 415-416: Extracts `top_p`, `min_p` from each `sampling_params`
- Lines 454-460: Replicates these scalars into tensor arrays matching sample lengths
- Returns tensors:
  - `top_ps`: torch.Tensor - one value per sample token
  - `top_ks`: torch.Tensor - one value per sample token
  - `min_ps`: torch.Tensor - one value per sample token
  - `temperatures`: torch.Tensor

### 6. **Model Executor Layer → Sampler**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 225-289)

**Flow:**
1. `Sampler.forward()` receives logits and `SamplingMetadata`
2. Calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` to convert params to GPU tensors
3. Applies sampling operations in sequence:
   - Line 282: Temperature scaling: `logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))`
   - Line 285-286: Top-k/top-p filtering via `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424)
   - Line 289: Min-p filtering via `_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441)
4. Computes probabilities and performs multinomial sampling

**Detailed Sampler Operations:**
- `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424):
  - Uses `sampling_tensors.top_ps` and `sampling_tensors.top_ks`
  - Sets logits to -inf for tokens not in top-k/top-p
- `_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441):
  - Uses `sampling_tensors.min_ps`
  - Filters tokens below min_p threshold

### 7. **V1 Engine Layer (Alternative Path)**

**Files:**
- `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` (lines 120-320)
- `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-43)
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 90-137)

**Flow (V1):**
1. `InputBatch` maintains CPU arrays for sampling params:
   - Line 133-140: `top_p_cpu`, `top_p_cpu_tensor`
   - Line 143-150: `top_k_cpu`, `top_k_cpu_tensor`
   - Line 122-129: `temperature_cpu`, `temperature_cpu_tensor`
2. When adding requests (line 309-320):
   ```python
   self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
   self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
   self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
   ```
3. Transfers to GPU via `copy_slice()` (line 603-610)
4. `SamplingMetadata` passes to V1 Sampler with these tensors
5. V1 Sampler applies filters using `TopKTopPSampler` (line 121-126)

### 8. **All Files Referencing top_p, min_p, or top_k**

Based on grep results, here are the primary files:

| Category | Files |
|----------|-------|
| **API/Protocol** | `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` |
| **Core Params** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` |
| **Metadata** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` |
| **Sampler** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`, `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` |
| **Workers** | `vllm/worker/model_runner.py`, `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` |
| **Engine** | `vllm/engine/llm_engine.py`, `vllm/v1/engine/llm_engine.py` |

### 9. **Test Files for Sampling Parameters**

Located in:
- `/tests/test_sampling_params.py` - Basic SamplingParams validation and OpenAI request conversion
- `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` - V1 end-to-end sampling tests
- `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py` - V1 sampler unit tests
- `/tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/top-p sampler tests
- `/tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - V0 sampler tests

### 10. **Data Flow Summary Diagram**

```
API Request (top_p, top_k, min_p, temperature)
    ↓
OpenAI Request Object (ChatCompletionRequest/CompletionRequest)
    ↓
SamplingParams.from_optional() [/vllm/sampling_params.py]
    ↓
Engine.generate(SamplingParams) [/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py]
    ↓
SequenceGroup(sampling_params) [/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py]
    ↓
Scheduler → SequenceGroupMetadata(sampling_params)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare() [/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py]
    ↓
SequenceGroupToSample(sampling_params)
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata() [Extract to GPU tensors]
    ↓
Sampler.forward() [/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py]
    ├→ _apply_top_k_top_p(top_ps, top_ks)
    ├→ _apply_min_p(min_ps)
    └→ Multinomial Sampling
```

All sampling parameters are preserved throughout the entire pipeline, from user API request through to the actual sampling kernel operations on the GPU.

## 3. Top-P and Top-K Filtering Logic

### **V0 Implementation** (_apply_top_k_top_p in sampler.py)
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Flow in V0 forward():**
1. Apply temperature scaling (line 281)
2. Apply top_k_top_p (line 284-285) if `do_top_p_top_k` is True
3. Apply min_p (line 287-288) if `do_min_p` is True
4. Compute probabilities via softmax (line 292)
5. Sample from the filtered distribution (line 297-304)

### **V1 Implementation** (vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py)

**apply_top_k_top_p()** - Main filtering function
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k.
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p.
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**apply_top_k_only()** - Optimized for top-k only (avoids full sort)
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## 4. Min-P Filtering Logic

### **V0 Implementation** (_apply_min_p in sampler.py)
```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

**Algorithm:**
1. Convert logits to probabilities via softmax
2. Find maximum probability in each sequence: `top_probs = max(probs)`
3. Scale min_p by the max probability: `scaled_min_p = min_p * top_probs`
4. Filter tokens with prob < scaled_min_p by setting their logits to -inf
5. Result: Only tokens with probability >= (min_p * max_prob) remain

### **V1 Implementation** (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py - MinPLogitsProcessor)
```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool, device):
        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs,), ...)
        self.min_p_device = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), ...)
        self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

---

## 5. Parameter Flow: SamplingParams → Filtering

The flow from user-specified parameters to actual filtering:

```
SamplingParams (vllm/sampling_params.py)
├── top_p: float = 1.0        # cumulative probability threshold
├── top_k: int = 0            # number of top tokens to keep
├── min_p: float = 0.0        # minimum probability relative to max
├── temperature: float = 1.0
├── presence_penalty: float = 0.0
├── frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
└── repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
                ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
├── Creates batched tensors for all parameters
├── Determines: do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_penalties
└── Returns per-token parameter tensors
                ↓
Sampler.forward()  (V0: sampler.py, V1: sampler.py)
├── Apply temperature scaling: logits / temperature
├── Apply top_k_top_p: if do_top_p_top_k
│   └── _apply_top_k_top_p() or apply_top_k_top_p()
├── Apply min_p: if do_min_p
│   └── _apply_min_p() or MinPLogitsProcessor.apply()
├── Compute softmax: probs = softmax(logits)
└── Sample: tokens = multinomial_sample(probs)
```

---

## 6. CUDA/GPU Kernels

### **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/csrc/sampler.cu`

The CUDA kernel only implements penalty application (repetition penalties), not top-k/top-p/min-p filtering:

```cuda
__global__ void apply_repetition_penalties_kernel(
    scalar_t* __restrict__ logits,         // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const bool* __restrict__ prompt_mask,  // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const bool* __restrict__ output_mask,  // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ repetition_penalties,  // [num_seqs]
    const int num_seqs, const int vocab_size, const int tile_size)
```

**Important**: Top-k, top-p, and min-p filtering are **pure PyTorch operations** (no custom CUDA kernels in vLLM's sampler.cu). However, FlashInfer library is optionally used for optimized top-k/top-p sampling via `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits()`.

---

## 7. Key Implementation Details

| Aspect | V0 | V1 |
|--------|----|----|
| **Top-K Logic** | Sort logits, mask tokens outside top-k | Sort logits or use topk for optimization |
| **Top-P Logic** | Sort logits, compute cumsum of probs, mask tokens where cumsum > 1-p | Same as V0, in apply_top_k_top_p or topk_topp_sampler |
| **Min-P Logic** | Compute softmax, scale min_p by max prob, mask tokens below threshold | In MinPLogitsProcessor, same algorithm |
| **Filtering Approach** | Direct masking with -inf | Direct masking with -inf |
| **Optional Optimization** | Can use FlashInfer if enabled | Uses FlashInfer by default (V1) unless disabled |
| **Per-Request Params** | Batched in SamplingTensors | Batched in logits processor state |

---

## 8. File Paths Summary

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| SamplingParams definition | `/vllm/sampling_params.py` |
| SamplingMetadata & SamplingTensors | `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` |
| V0 Sampler (legacy) | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` |
| V0 Top-K/Top-P filtering | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 397-424) |
| V0 Min-P filtering | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 427-441) |
| V1 Sampler | `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` |
| V1 Top-K/Top-P filtering | `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` |
| V1 Min-P filtering | `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (MinPLogitsProcessor class) |
| CUDA penalties kernel | `/csrc/sampler.cu` |

    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## 4. Min-P Filtering Logic

### **V0 Implementation** (_apply_min_p in sampler.py)
```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

**Algorithm:**
1. Convert logits to probabilities via softmax
2. Find maximum probability in each sequence: `top_probs = max(probs)`
3. Scale min_p by the max probability: `scaled_min_p = min_p * top_probs`
4. Filter tokens with prob < scaled_min_p by setting their logits to -inf
5. Result: Only tokens with probability >= (min_p * max_prob) remain

### **V1 Implementation** (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py - MinPLogitsProcessor)
```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool, device):
        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs,), ...)
        self.min_p_device = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), ...)
        self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

---

## 5. Parameter Flow: SamplingParams → Filtering

The flow from user-specified parameters to actual filtering:

```
SamplingParams (vllm/sampling_params.py)
├── top_p: float = 1.0        # cumulative probability threshold
├── top_k: int = 0            # number of top tokens to keep
├── min_p: float = 0.0        # minimum probability relative to max
├── temperature: float = 1.0
├── presence_penalty: float = 0.0
├── frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
└── repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
                ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
├── Creates batched tensors for all parameters
├── Determines: do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_penalties
└── Returns per-token parameter tensors
                ↓
Sampler.forward()  (V0: sampler.py, V1: sampler.py)
├── Apply temperature scaling: logits / temperature
├── Apply top_k_top_p: if do_top_p_top_k
│   └── _apply_top_k_top_p() or apply_top_k_top_p()
├── Apply min_p: if do_min_p
│   └── _apply_min_p() or MinPLogitsProcessor.apply()
├── Compute softmax: probs = softmax(logits)
└── Sample: tokens = multinomial_sample(probs)
```

---

## 6. CUDA/GPU Kernels

### **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496249420-qrgdii/csrc/sampler.cu`

The CUDA kernel only implements penalty application (repetition penalties), not top-k/top-p/min-p filtering:

```cuda
__global__ void apply_repetition_penalties_kernel(
    scalar_t* __restrict__ logits,         // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const bool* __restrict__ prompt_mask,  // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const bool* __restrict__ output_mask,  // [num_seqs, vocab_size]
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ repetition_penalties,  // [num_seqs]
    const int num_seqs, const int vocab_size, const int tile_size)
```

**Important**: Top-k, top-p, and min-p filtering are **pure PyTorch operations** (no custom CUDA kernels in vLLM's sampler.cu). However, FlashInfer library is optionally used for optimized top-k/top-p sampling via `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits()`.

---

## 7. Key Implementation Details

| Aspect | V0 | V1 |
|--------|----|----|
| **Top-K Logic** | Sort logits, mask tokens outside top-k | Sort logits or use topk for optimization |
| **Top-P Logic** | Sort logits, compute cumsum of probs, mask tokens where cumsum > 1-p | Same as V0, in apply_top_k_top_p or topk_topp_sampler |
| **Min-P Logic** | Compute softmax, scale min_p by max prob, mask tokens below threshold | In MinPLogitsProcessor, same algorithm |
| **Filtering Approach** | Direct masking with -inf | Direct masking with -inf |
| **Optional Optimization** | Can use FlashInfer if enabled | Uses FlashInfer by default (V1) unless disabled |
| **Per-Request Params** | Batched in SamplingTensors | Batched in logits processor state |

---

## 8. File Paths Summary

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| SamplingParams definition | `/vllm/sampling_params.py` |
| SamplingMetadata & SamplingTensors | `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` |
| V0 Sampler (legacy) | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` |
| V0 Top-K/Top-P filtering | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 397-424) |
| V0 Min-P filtering | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 427-441) |
| V1 Sampler | `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` |
| V1 Top-K/Top-P filtering | `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` |
| V1 Min-P filtering | `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (MinPLogitsProcessor class) |
| CUDA penalties kernel | `/csrc/sampler.cu` |

384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
386	
387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
415	            raise ValueError(
416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
420	        if self.top_k < -1:
421	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
424	            raise TypeError(
425	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
429	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
430	            raise ValueError(
431	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
432	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
433	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
434	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
435	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
436	            raise ValueError(
437	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
438	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
439	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
440	            raise ValueError(
441	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
449	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
450	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
454	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
463	
464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
465	        if self.n > 1:
466	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
467	                             f"got {self.n}.")
468	
469	    def update_from_generation_config(
470	            self,
471	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
472	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
473	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
474	
475	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
476	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
477	            # min_tokens processing.
478	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
479	
480	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
481	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
482	            # it can be either int or list of int
483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
550	
551	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
553	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
554	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
555	        """
556	
557	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
562	
563	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
566	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
567	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
568	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
569	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
570	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
573	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
574	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
575	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
576	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
577	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
578	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
579	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
580	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
581	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
582	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
583	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
584	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
585	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
586	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
587	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
588	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
589	
590	
591	class BeamSearchParams(
592	        msgspec.Struct,
593	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
594	        # required for @cached_property.
595	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
596	    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
597	    beam_width: int
598	    max_tokens: int
599	    ignore_eos: bool = False
600	    temperature: float = 0.0
601	    length_penalty: float = 1.0
602	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
603
